Conceptual entity mapping - Source CUSTOMER table maps to Target Customer table with significant structural changes. Source uses char(3) composite key; Target uses uniqueidentifier single key. Address fields moved from inline to BusinessPartyId reference. Enhanced audit fields in Target.

Conceptual entity mapping - Source CLIENT concept (char(4) in DBFILE) maps to Target Entity table. Target introduces hierarchical relationship with Enterprise and enhanced entity management capabilities including fiscal periods and accounting basis.
Architectural pattern change - Source stores address fields inline (CUSTOMER_ADDR
column7: CUSTOMER_ZIP). Target normalizes addresses through BusinessPartyId reference to separate party management system.

Attribute Mapping Notes: Data type modernization - Source predominantly uses char fixed-length and varchar. Target uses nvarchar for Unicode support and varchar for codes. Requires character set conversion and length adjustments.
Primary key strategy change - Source uses int and char composite keys. Target uses uniqueidentifier (GUID) for all primary keys. Requires key transformation and surrogate key generation.
Temporal data type upgrade - Source uses datetime with 3.33ms precision. Target uses datetime2(7) with 100ns precision for enhanced temporal accuracy.
Audit capability enhancement - Source has limited audit fields (CHANGED
column10: DeactivatedDate) plus EntityHistory table for field-level change tracking.

Attribute Mapping Notes: Schema architecture evolution - Source has flat table structure. Target introduces Enterprise > Customer > Entity hierarchy with normalized reference data (EntityType

Security model change - Source has explicit CUSTOMER_SECURITY table for user-customer access control. Target schema provided does not include equivalent security tables suggesting security handled externally or in separate schema.

Document management pattern change - Source has explicit DBFILE and DBFILE_DETAIL tables for document storage with inline content. Target uses DocumentId reference suggesting document management externalized to separate document service or repository.
